questionHtml:
A, B and C started a business in partnership Initially, A invested ₹29,000, while B and C invested ₹25,000 each. After 4 months, A withdrew ₹3,000. After 2 more months, C invested ₹12,000 more. Find the share of C (in ₹) in the profit of ₹33,200 at the end of the year.

  1. 10,800

  2. 10,000

  3. 11,067

  4. 12,400
subject: Mathematics

questionHtml:
A and B start a business, A invest 3313% of the total capital and B invests the remaining. If the total profit at the end of the year ₹1,62,000 then B’s share (in ₹) is 

  1. 1,08,000

  2. 1,12,000

  3. 1,20,000

  4. 54,000
subject: Mathematics

questionHtml:
A,B and C invested ₹40,000, ₹48000 and ₹80,000 respectively , for a business at the start of the year. After six months for the remaining time of the year, A added ₹4000, B added ₹4000 and C withdrew ₹4000 every month. If the total profit is ₹6,72,000. Then what is the share of C ( in ₹) ?

  1. 1,96,750

  2. 1,80,480

  3. 2,11,200

  4. 2,80,320
subject: Mathematics

questionHtml:
A,B and C start a business. A invests 3313%  of the total capital, B invests 25% of the remaining and C, the rest. If the total profit at the end of the year is ₹2,19,000. Then A share ( in ₹) is. 

  1. 65000

  2. 71000

  3. 73000

  4. 79000
subject: Mathematics

questionHtml:
P and Q start a shop with a capital of ₹1,50,000 and ₹4,50,000, respectively. After a year, out of the profit of ₹1,60,000, P gets his share of profit plus some money that is not a part of the profit, as his salary. If P gets a total of ₹70,000, what is the salary (in ₹) he received?

  1. 40,000

  2. 30,000

  3. 25,000

  4. 50,000
subject: Mathematics

questionHtml:
A, B and C start a business. A invests 3313%  of the total capital, B invests 25% of the remaining, and C invests the rest. If The total profit at the end of the year is ₹1,86,000, then A's share of the profit (in ₹ )is

  1. 62,000

  2. 64,000

  3. 61,000

  4. 59,000
subject: Mathematics

questionHtml:
A and B had a joint business in which A invested Rs. 60,000 in the business for one year. After 3 months B invested Rs. 80,000. At the beginning of the second year, A invested Rs. 30,000 more and B withdrew Rs. 5,000. At the end of two years, profit earned by A is Rs. 35,880. What is the profit (in Rs.) earned by B, if they distributed half of the total profit equally and rest in the capital ratio?

  1. 69,920

  2. 38,060

  3. 34,040

  4. 58,940
subject: Mathematics

questionHtml:
A and B entered into a partnership with certain investment At the end of 8 months A withdrew and collected back his money. A and B received profit in the ratio of 5:9 at the end of the year. If B had invested ₹36,000 then how much capital A had invested(in ₹)

  1. 25,000

  2. 30,000

  3. 20,000

  4. 36,000
subject: Mathematics

questionHtml:
Three partners shared the profit in a business in the proportion 9 : 8 : 11. They invested their capitals for 4 month,6months and18 months, respectively .What was the ratio of their capitals?

  1. 81:48:22

  2. 27:48:22

  3. 81:16:66

  4. 27:16:66
subject: Mathematics

questionHtml:
Person A started a business by investing Rs. 65,000. After a few months, B joined him by investing Rs. 50,000. Three months after joining of B, C joined the two with an Investment of ₹55,000. At the end of the year. A got 50% of profit as his share. For How many months did A alone finance the business?

  1. 2

  2. 3

  3. 5

  4. 4
subject: Mathematics

questionHtml:
A and B entered into a partnership with investment in the ratio 3:5 after a few months , A withdrew and collected back his money . At the end of the year , they received profit in ratio 2:5 after how many months did A withdraw ?

  1. 7

  2. 9

  3. 8

  4. 6
subject: Mathematics

questionHtml:
Three partners X, Y and Z started their business by investing ₹40,000 , ₹38,000 and ₹30,000, respectively. After 6 months. X and Z make additional investments of ₹20,000 and ₹15,000 respectively. Whereas Y withdrew ₹8,000. Find the share of Y (in ₹) in the total profit of ₹38,880 made at the end of the year.

  1. 10,880

  2. 10,950

  3. 9,800

  4. 10,200
subject: Mathematics
